name: compile_spine_packet_shadow
outcome_goal: Compile shadow execution packets with provenance and inspection so migrated LLM jobs stop treating raw prompt text as sufficient authority before the hard cutover.
anti_requirements: Do not switch execution to packet-only behavior in this packet.
Do not replan explicit jobs or widen into unrelated task_assembler planning paths.
Do not add mixed hidden fallbacks that fabricate packet provenance.

jobs.label: execution_packet_contract_store
jobs.outcome_goal: Add durable ExecutionPacket persistence and provenance lineage keyed to definition and plan revisions.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: Persist packet truth first; do not hand-wave provenance into logs only.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Persist execution packets and their provenance before the runtime cutover.

TASK:
Add durable `ExecutionPacket` storage and provenance lineage keyed to `definition_revision` and `plan_revision`. The packet must capture exact model messages, reference bindings, capability bindings, `verify_refs`, packet revision, and the authority/file inputs used to compile it. Wire that persistence into the workflow runtime recording path so packet truth is inspectable and replayable.

CONSTRAINTS:
Do not cut execution over to packet-only behavior. Do not treat receipt text alone as sufficient provenance. Keep explicit jobs non-replanning.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Migration(s), runtime persistence wiring, and receipt/provenance documentation for shadow execution packets.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- Execution packets persist with revision lineage.
- Packet provenance records the exact authority rows and context inputs used.
- Packet truth is available to runtime inspection without changing job outcomes.
- Tests cover persistence and submission/read seams.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not switch adapters to packet-only execution in this job.

jobs.label: shadow_packet_compiler
jobs.outcome_goal: Compile a shadow execution packet for every migrated LLM job before adapter execution.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: Hydration only; no hidden replanning and no fabricated provenance on failure.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Compile shadow execution packets ahead of adapter execution on the migrated runtime seam.

TASK:
Upgrade the context/compiler side of the workflow graph so every migrated LLM job builds a shadow execution packet before execution. The packet must include the compiled system/user messages, scope reads/writes, reference bindings, capability bindings, verifier refs, and provenance. Reuse plan/definition revisions where present, and preserve the rule that explicit `jobs[]` specs are hydrated but not replanned.

CONSTRAINTS:
Do not switch adapters to packet-only execution yet. Do not widen into unrelated `task_assembler` planner logic. Missing packet authority should fail clearly on the shadow path rather than fabricating values.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Runtime graph and compiler changes that create and persist shadow packets for migrated LLM jobs.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- Each migrated LLM job builds a shadow packet before execution.
- Packet provenance captures exact context and authority inputs.
- Shadow packet creation does not change job outcomes yet.
- Tests prove deterministic packet assembly and fail-closed behavior.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not remove raw prompt execution from adapters in this packet.

jobs.label: packet_inspection_surface
jobs.outcome_goal: Expose packet and provenance inspection through existing read surfaces so runs can be audited without shell spelunking.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: No hidden packet truth; if it executed, it should be inspectable.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Make shadow execution packets inspectable through the supported read surfaces.

TASK:
Expose packet inspection through the existing workflow query and operator read surfaces so a run can show exact model inputs, authority bindings, `verify_refs`, packet revision, and packet drift versus execution. Keep the surface read-only and operator-friendly; do not require shell log scraping to see packet provenance.

CONSTRAINTS:
Do not redesign the UI. Do not switch execution to packet-only behavior here. Keep packet inspection aligned to existing workflow/operator read contracts.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Read-surface endpoints or payload enrichments plus tests that prove packet/provenance inspection works on the shadow path.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- A run can surface its packet revision, bindings, and provenance through existing reads.
- Packet drift is inspectable without changing execution behavior.
- Tests prove the new read model shape.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not turn packet inspection into a new write surface or a UI redesign.
